title: buprestis gerardi
side_effects: Buprestis gerardi can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Ingesting its leaves or flowers can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can also cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue in some individuals. Furthermore, improper use of Buprestis gerardi can trigger severe reactions, including anaphylaxis, in people with pre-existing allergies or sensitivities.
